why_now: The Bad Epoll flaw is a high-risk vulnerability that can be exploited to gain root access on Linux and Android systems.
recommended_actions_24h: Apply the available patch to the Linux kernel
Isolate vulnerable systems from the internet
recommended_actions_7d: Update Android devices to the latest version
title: CVE-2026-46242: Linux Kernel Privilege Escalation
summary: [P1] A newly disclosed Linux kernel flaw called Bad Epoll lets an ordinary user with no special access take full control of a machine as root. The flaw affects Linux and Android, and a patch is currently available. Why now: The Bad Epoll flaw is a high-risk vulnerability that can be exploited to gain root access on Linux and Android systems. (confidence: 0.90)
classification_reasoning: The Bad Epoll flaw is a privilege escalation vulnerability that affects the Linux kernel, which is a foundational component of Linux and Android systems.

primary.title: New "Bad Epoll" Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Unprivileged Users Gain Root, Hits Android

enrichment.lede: New "Bad Epoll" Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Unprivileged Users Gain Root, Hits Android.
